name: How can I make a small bathroom feel bigger during a remodel?
headline: How can I make a small bathroom feel bigger during a remodel?

mainEntity.name: How can I make a small bathroom feel bigger during a remodel?
mainEntity.text: How can I make a small bathroom feel bigger during a remodel?

acceptedAnswer.text: If your bathroom is small, there are several smart ways to make it feel bigger without moving walls. First, choose lighter colors on walls and tile; whites, creams, and soft grays reflect light and open up the room. Large-format tiles with thin grout lines can also make the floor and walls look less busy. Using a clear glass shower door instead of an opaque curtain helps the eye see the full space. A wall-hung vanity or a vanity with open legs can show more floor, which makes the room feel larger. Recessed medicine cabinets and built-in niches in the shower add storage without sticking out into the room. Good lighting is key. Combine overhead lighting with a vanity light that doesn’t cast harsh shadows. A big, simple mirror over the sink bounces light and visually doubles the space. In older homes around Daly City and San Bruno, small bathrooms sometimes feel cramped because of bulky fixtures. Swapping an oversized vanity for a slimmer one, or using a corner sink or toilet, can free up floor space. A common pitfall is trying to cram in too many design features, patterns, or shelves. That can make a small room feel cluttered and tight. Keep the design clean, limit the number of tile patterns, and focus on smart storage and light to get the most out of a small bathroom.
